name: GaugeFlow substrate-v2 decoration-only qualification v2
objective: Qualify a geometry-aware categorical site-decoration substrate before any tensor-conditioned flow experiment. This is a fixed-geometry endpoint-ID control, not a rerun, amendment, or replacement of A5--A11.

supersedes.reason: The completed v1 runner did not recompute the model on relabelled inputs for its node-relabeling criterion, and its FP32 exact-enumeration reduction was order-sensitive after score saturation. The v1 artifacts remain preserved but are invalid for promotion. This v2 changes neither panel, capacity, optimizer, seeds, steps, thresholds, nor scientific target; it makes the required probability-vector equivariance test real and numerically stable.

historical_evidence_is_immutable.instruction: Gate A through A11.0 reports, thresholds, checkpoints, data split, and conclusions remain frozen. A11-Q1 remains unstarted and is not changed by this protocol.
historical_evidence_is_immutable.new_protocol_reason: This protocol supplies true graph-level composition counts only to isolate whether periodic geometry can make sites chemically distinguishable. It is intentionally different from A11-Q1, where composition must be generated.

scope.state: fixed true periodic fractional geometry and row-vector lattice; all atom species are absorbing-mask tokens
scope.vocabulary: dense chemical tokens 0..117 for atomic numbers 1..118; internal mask token 118; no untrained chemical class 0
scope.not_a_production_claim: Supplying counts is allowed only for this isolated scorer qualification and does not establish unconditional composition generation.

architectures.purpose: Frozen-style scalar reference: normalized local direction messages without metric radial features or vector-state invariants.
Adds periodic closest-image distance/RBF features to every scalar message.
Adds equivariant vector channels; scalar messages consume their dot-product/norm invariants as well as RBF metric geometry.
Applied identically to every scorer: exact count-constrained likelihood under the proper-SO(3) residual automorphism group, rather than a fixed-CIF row loss.

assignment.training_law: For site--species scores C[i,k] and supplied qualification counts n, enumerate each unique Y in A(n), set S(Y)=sum_i C[i,Y_i], and normalize exp(S(Y)) exactly. The target likelihood is summed only over UniqueOrbit_{Gamma_t}(Y). Exact enumerated energies are accumulated in float64; gradients remain connected to the model scores.
assignment.sampling: assignment-level categorical or Gumbel-max on the complete exact support; repeated species slots are never duplicated.
assignment.automorphism_scope: proper-SO(3) in the production qualification; full-O(3) is diagnostic only; residual groups are recomputed from the current mask/reveal state.
assignment.node_relabeling_audit: Run a separate model forward pass on a fixed pre-registered site permutation [2,0,3,1]. Compare the entire exact-assignment log-probability vector after transforming assignment labelings. This is not a score-matrix-only algebraic check.
assignment.optional_exchangeable_node_noise: not enabled. Assignment-level Gumbel is the initial exchangeable symmetry-breaking mechanism. Any node-noise comparison requires a new versioned protocol.

data_and_repetitions.panel: InN/BN, JVASP-1180/JVASP-22673, from the attested local-pinned TensorOrbit-JARVIS-v2 raw build
data_and_repetitions.common_noise: fixed assignment-level Gumbels are reused for counterfactual endpoint-ID samples

required_diagnostics: fixed-CIF site accuracy, reported only as a diagnostic
assignment entropy and probability of every equivalent labeling
residual automorphism group size along mask reveal states
periodic edge displacement, closest-image shift, and distance sanity checks
permutation consistency of scorer logits and complete assignment probability vector from a fresh relabelled model forward
promotion_rule: Only if the exact-quotient geometry-aware scorer meets every primary threshold on every pre-registered seed may a new, separately versioned composition-generator qualification be proposed. Tensor conditioning is still not restored by this protocol.
